register_notice: Append-only. Entries are never deleted or edited in place; corrections are new entries linked via superseded_by.

entries.document_title: ASIC issues early observations on sustainability reporting ahead of 30 June 2026
entries.summary: ASIC published early observations from a desktop review of sustainability reports prepared under Chapter 2M of the Corporations Act 2001, to assist reporting entities ahead of the 30 June 2026 reporting season. Published 18 May 2026.
entries.verification_notes: Source URL located and cross-confirmed via two independent search queries returning consistent title and content matching this exact ASIC news item. Publication date and full article text (the 'Published 18 May 2026' byline, the six early-observations items, and the 'next steps' section referencing 26-098MR) were then directly confirmed on 2026-07-21 from the live page text, supplied by the operator (the sandbox itself still cannot fetch asic.gov.au directly). document_hash remains unset since this was a text capture, not a raw byte fetch — the GitHub Actions runner's hash-capture pass can still complete that.

proof_of_fact.rationale: Source URL independently corroborated via search cross-reference, but document_hash has not yet been computed from a direct fetch — full Proof of Fact requires that step.

entries.document_title: ASIC guidance on sustainability reporting obligations (Chapter 2M, Corporations Act 2001 / AASB S2)
entries.summary: ASIC's sustainability reporting regulatory resources hub, covering mandatory reporting obligations under Chapter 2M of the Corporations Act 2001 and the Australian Sustainability Reporting Standard AASB S2 Climate-related Disclosures, phased in across three entity groups starting with financial years commencing on or after 1 January 2025.
entries.verification_notes: Source URL located and cross-confirmed via independent search queries returning consistent content describing these obligations. Full page text (Chapter 2M, s292A thresholds, ASIC's administration role) was then directly confirmed on 2026-07-21 from the live page text, supplied by the operator. This is an evergreen resource page with no publication byline. document_hash remains unset since this was a text capture, not a raw byte fetch.

proof_of_fact.rationale: Source URL independently corroborated via search cross-reference, but document_hash has not yet been computed from a direct fetch — full Proof of Fact requires that step.

entries.document_title: ASIC issues early observations on sustainability reporting ahead of 30 June 2026 (259 reports lodged: 34 listed, 225 unlisted)
entries.summary: Per ASIC's early observations release (published 18 May 2026), 259 sustainability reports were lodged for the financial year ending 31 December 2025 (34 from listed entities, 225 from unlisted entities), reviewed for compliance with the Corporations Act and AASB S2.
entries.verification_notes: Same source document as E-001. Source URL and article text cross-confirmed via independent search queries and, on 2026-07-21, a direct text capture of the live page supplied by the operator confirming the article itself, its date, and 'Figure 1 sets out an overview of the total number of sustainability reports lodged with ASIC as of 6 May 2026'. IMPORTANT CAVEAT: the specific 259/34/225 figures live in that Figure 1 image, not in the plain text captured — the direct capture confirms the figure exists and its data-as-of date (6 May 2026), but does not itself independently confirm the exact numbers, which still rest on the earlier search-engine corroboration only. Treat those specific numbers as MEDIUM rather than fully document-confirmed until the image or its 'text version' link is captured directly.

proof_of_fact.rationale: Source URL and specific figures independently corroborated via search cross-reference, but document_hash has not yet been computed from a direct fetch — full Proof of Fact requires that step.

entries.document_title: 26-098MR ASIC sets financial reporting, audit and sustainability focus areas for FY 2026–27
entries.summary: ASIC numbered media release (26-098MR) setting out its financial reporting, audit and sustainability reporting focus areas for FY2026-27, including continued administration of the mandatory climate reporting framework for Group 1 entities and engagement with large audit firms on assurance methodologies.
entries.verification_notes: Source URL located and cross-confirmed via independent search; the '26-098MR' numbered-release format is ASIC's standard media release identifier. Direct fetch to compute document_hash is blocked by this session's sandbox network policy.

proof_of_fact.rationale: Source URL independently corroborated via search cross-reference, but document_hash has not yet been computed from a direct fetch.

entries.document_title: 25-051MR ASIC issues sustainability reporting regulatory guide (Regulatory Guide 280)
entries.summary: ASIC issued Regulatory Guide 280 (Sustainability reporting) on 31 March 2025 (RG 280 filename confirms 'published-31-march-2025'), setting out ASIC's guidance for entities preparing sustainability reports under Chapter 2M of the Corporations Act 2001.
entries.verification_notes: Source URL and publication date corroborated via independent search, including the RG 280 PDF's own filename (rg280-published-31-march-2025.pdf). Direct fetch to compute document_hash is blocked by this session's sandbox network policy.

proof_of_fact.rationale: Source URL and date independently corroborated via search cross-reference and the document filename itself, but document_hash has not yet been computed from a direct fetch.

entries.document_title: Australian Securities and Investments Commission's Regulation of Registered Company Auditors (Auditor-General Report No. 10 of 2025-26)
entries.summary: ANAO performance audit of ASIC's regulation of registered company auditors generally. Search results note the responsible ASIC branch was renamed 'Sustainability, Financial Reporting and Audit' in February 2025, but the audit itself is not sustainability-reporting-specific.
entries.verification_notes: Source URL confirmed via search. Held rather than Accepted: relevance to this case's specific scope (mandatory sustainability reporting) is indirect — the audit covers ASIC's auditor-regulation function broadly, not sustainability report assurance specifically. Requires reading the full report to confirm how directly it bears on this case before treating findings from it as case-specific evidence.

proof_of_fact.rationale: Source URL confirmed, but relevance scope and document_hash are both unconfirmed.

entries.document_title: Treasury Laws Amendment (Financial Market Infrastructure and Other Measures) Bill 2024
entries.summary: The Parliament of Australia bill page for the legislation that introduced Australia's mandatory climate-related financial disclosure regime, amending the Corporations Act 2001 to require sustainability reports under Chapter 2M. Received royal assent 17 September 2024, phased in from 1 January 2025.
entries.verification_notes: Source URL located and confirmed via independent search (bId=r7176 matches the official Parliament bill tracking ID). Direct fetch to compute document_hash is blocked by this session's sandbox network policy.

proof_of_fact.rationale: Source URL independently corroborated via search cross-reference, but document_hash has not yet been computed from a direct fetch.

entries.document_title: Treasury Laws Amendment (Financial Market Infrastructure and Other Measures) Act 2024 (No. 87, 2024)
entries.summary: The enacted Act (No. 87, 2024), as registered on the Federal Register of Legislation, that inserted the mandatory climate-related financial disclosure regime into the Corporations Act 2001. Received royal assent 17 September 2024.
entries.verification_notes: Source URL and Act number/register ID (C2024A00087) confirmed via independent search. Direct fetch to compute document_hash is blocked by this session's sandbox network policy.

entries.summary: ASIC newsletter (Issue 4, reported as May 2026) covering reporting and audit developments including early observations on sustainability reporting, the ASIC/AASB sustainability reporting webinar series, FY2026-27 focus areas, infringement notices for late lodgement, and updated FAQs on auditing and assurance requirements for sustainability reports.

entries.document_title: Sustainability reporting and audit relief decisions register
entries.summary: ASIC's public register of decisions on relief applications under Chapter 2M of the Corporations Act relating to sustainability reporting, updated quarterly. Improves transparency of ASIC's discretionary relief decisions and the factors it considers.
entries.verification_notes: Source URL located and confirmed via independent search; directly on-topic for this case (ASIC's administration of the mandatory sustainability reporting regime). Direct fetch to compute document_hash is blocked by this session's sandbox network policy.

entries.document_title: First sustainability reporting educational modules released to assist smaller companies
entries.summary: ASIC, with the AASB, released the first sustainability-reporting e-learning modules (reported: first three of eight modules, PDF versions issued December 2025) aimed at smaller reporting entities and suppliers to reporting entities, covering the Chapter 2M framework, climate change basics, and climate-related physical risks.
entries.verification_notes: Source URL located and confirmed via independent search; directly on-topic for this case. Direct fetch to compute document_hash is blocked by this session's sandbox network policy.

proof_of_fact.rationale: Source URL independently corroborated via search cross-reference, but document_hash has not yet been computed from a direct fetch.

entries.document_title: Addressing financial system climate risk: A view from the regulator (ASIC speech)
entries.summary: An official ASIC speech addressing climate-related financial system risk from the regulator's perspective. Recorded as HELD rather than ACCEPTED: a speech carries the regulator's own framing/commentary rather than a discrete verifiable fact like a data point or lodgement figure, so it is retained as a primary source but not cited for any specific substantive claim until its content is read in full.
entries.verification_notes: Source URL located and confirmed via independent search. Held pending full-text review to identify what, if any, specific factual claims it should be cited for; document_hash also not yet computed (direct fetch blocked by this session's sandbox network policy).

proof_of_fact.rationale: Source URL confirmed, but this speech's relevance is recorded at the 'primary source exists' level only, and document_hash has not yet been computed from a direct fetch.
